_meta.description: Machine-readable HTTP contract fixtures for POST /ask and POST /session/{id}/ask. Each fixture specifies the full request shape, the invariants that must hold in the response, and the stability level of each invariant. This file is the canonical source of truth for downstream consumers building against the FPL grounded assistant HTTP API.

stability_levels.stable: Part of the stable public contract. Changes are breaking and must be documented with a phase label.
stability_levels.debug_only: Only present when the request includes 'debug: true'. Not part of the stable response contract for production callers. Do not gate production logic on debug fields.
stability_levels.conditional: Present only for specific intent/outcome combinations. Always null otherwise.

debug.description: When true, populates the 'debug' bundle. Not for production use.

candidates_list.description: Explicit candidate list for rank_candidates intent. Each entry: {query: string}.

squad_context.description: Per-turn squad state. Used for budget and chip availability context.

intent_hint.description: Optional pre-classifier routing bias. Allowlisted to 7 values. Fires only on deterministic router miss. Invalid values are silently ignored.

POST /session/{session_id}/ask._note: Same request schema as POST /ask. Additionally accepts the session_id path parameter.

intent_hint.description: Per-turn only. Not stored in session state. Does not affect subsequent turns.

invariants: deterministic router wins: if route(question) succeeds, intent_hint is completely ignored
allowlisted only: values outside the allowlist are silently ignored
safe ignore: invalid hints never raise, never block, never error
pre-classifier: fires before the LLM classifier, without any LLM call
provider-neutral: no provider identity in the public contract
per-turn in sessions: hint is not stored in session state
classification_source audit: set to 'intent_hint' when hint fires; null for deterministic; 'llm_classifier' for LLM fallback (debug bundle only)

orch_outcome_contract.note: Always present in the HTTP response body. JSON null when orch was not attempted (orch flag OFF). String value otherwise.

values.null: Orchestration not attempted (no API client, orch disabled, or sub-call inside multi-intent turn)
values.ok: Orchestrator succeeded; its answer was used to build the response
values.no_client: Orch attempted; no Anthropic client available; fell back to deterministic path
values.llm_error: Orch attempted; LLM call raised exception; fell back to deterministic path
values.no_tool: Orchestrator returned no tool call; fell back to deterministic path
values.unknown_tool: Orchestrator called an unregistered tool; fell back to deterministic path
values.tool_error: Registered tool raised exception during execution; fell back to deterministic path
values.tool_result_error: Tool result could not be parsed into a valid FinalResponse; fell back to deterministic path

orch_outcome_contract.independence_invariant: orch_outcome is independent of outcome. A non-OK orch_outcome never changes outcome. outcome always reflects the deterministic result and is safe for routing decisions without inspecting orch_outcome.
orch_outcome_contract.override_invariant: squad_context overrides (budget_constraint, hit_warning, chip_unavailable) are applied identically regardless of orch_outcome. The same _apply_squad_overrides helper is called on both deterministic and orch-success paths.

response_conditional_fields.captain: populated for captain_score OK turns only; null otherwise
response_conditional_fields.captain_ranking: populated for rank_candidates OK turns only; null otherwise
response_conditional_fields.comparison: populated for compare_players OK turns only; null otherwise
response_conditional_fields.transfer: populated for transfer_advice OK turns only; null otherwise
response_conditional_fields.chip: populated for chip_advice OK turns only; null otherwise
response_conditional_fields.fixture_run: populated for player_fixture_run OK turns only; null otherwise
response_conditional_fields.differential: populated for differential_picks OK turns only; null otherwise
response_conditional_fields.sub_responses: populated for multi_intent turns only; null for single-intent turns

response_debug_only_fields.debug.response_text: deterministic grounded response text before LLM presentation
response_debug_only_fields.debug.llm_text: raw LLM output (may equal response_text if LLM not called)
response_debug_only_fields.debug.violations: list of review violation strings; empty when review passes
response_debug_only_fields.debug.prompt_used: user-turn prompt sent to the LLM
response_debug_only_fields.debug.model: model identifier used; 'none' when LLM not called
response_debug_only_fields.debug.classification_source: how intent was resolved: null=deterministic, 'intent_hint', or 'llm_classifier'

routing_trace_contract.surface: ask_v2() return value (not POST /ask or POST /session/{id}/ask)
routing_trace_contract.note: Additive, optional field. Always present in ask_v2() return values. Schema is pinned by ROUTING_TRACE_REQUIRED_KEYS and ROUTING_TRACE_OPTIONAL_KEYS in fpl_grounded_assistant.harness.

required_keys.classifier_called: bool — True iff classify_intent_llm() was invoked
required_keys.classifier_intent: str|null — LLM intent label, or null
required_keys.orchestrator_called: bool — True iff ask_orchestrated() was invoked
required_keys.orchestrator_tool_calls: list[str]|null — tools chosen by orchestrator, or null
required_keys.orchestrator_outcome: str|null — orchestrator OUTCOME_* constant, or null
required_keys.grounded: bool — True iff a deterministic tool ran end-to-end
required_keys.feature_flag_orch_enabled: bool — snapshot of FPL_ORCH_ENABLED at call time

optional_keys.expansion_text: str — prompt-expansion branch: canonical text produced
optional_keys.workflow_intent: str — prompt branches: prompt_registry workflow intent label
optional_keys.dispatched_tool: str — prompt-dispatch branch: tool name invoked

branch_values.route: plain text; route() succeeded on first try (fully deterministic)
branch_values.classifier_rewrite: plain text; route() missed, LLM rewrote, route() succeeded on rewrite
branch_values.orchestrator: plain text; all deterministic paths missed, orchestrator returned grounded tool call
branch_values.unsupported: no path produced a grounded answer

routing_trace_contract.pre_M5_note: For M3-M4 this field was debug-tier. Callers that depended on it during M3-M4 must re-validate against the M5 schema constants.

healthz_contract.note: Per-branch routing counters and graduation-criteria evaluation. Not a deployment readiness probe (use GET /ready for that). Counters are process-global and reset on restart.

graduation_criteria.reject_rate_lt_5: reject_rate < 0.05 (fewer than 5% of inputs fully unsupported)

ask_fixtures.description: Standard captain score query. Deterministic routing succeeds. No intent_hint needed. orch_outcome is null (no API client in test environment).

orch_outcome.note: null when orch not attempted (no API client); always present as a key in JSON response

captain.note: captain_score OK turn â€” captain field must be populated

ask_fixtures.description: Out-of-scope question. No route. Falls through to unsupported_intent.
Bare name does not route deterministically. Valid intent_hint fires pre-classifier and routes via canonical synthesis.
Routable question with a mismatched hint. Deterministic router succeeds â†’ hint is completely ignored.

debug.classification_source.note: null = deterministic routing; hint never fired

ask_fixtures.description: Bare name + invalid hint outside the allowlist. Hint silently ignored. Falls through to unsupported_intent.

supported.note: invalid hint silently ignored; no error raised

ask_fixtures.description: Supported intent but player not in registry.
Player name matches multiple entries. supported=True; outcome=ambiguous.
Missing required 'question' field. HTTP 422 â€” request validation error.

expected.note: HTTP status 422 is the contract signal for malformed requests. Body detail is unspecified.

ask_fixtures.description: Verify debug bundle populates all documented fields when debug=true.
orch_outcome is always a key in the JSON response body, even when null. Callers must not treat its absence as null â€” they must treat the key as always present.

orch_outcome.note: Key is always present. Value is null when orch not attempted, string when orch was attempted.

ask_fixtures.description: orch_outcome is independent of outcome. A non-OK orch_outcome (orch fell back) never changes outcome. outcome always reflects the deterministic result.
ask_fixtures.surface_note: Non-OK orch outcomes are only observable in environments with an API client where orch fires but fails. In environments without a client, orch_outcome=null and outcome=deterministic.

outcome.note: outcome always reflects deterministic result regardless of orch state

orch_outcome.independence_invariant: orch_outcome != 'ok' does NOT imply outcome != 'ok'. They are independent. Non-OK orch means orch fell back; outcome still reflects the deterministic answer.

ask_fixtures.description: squad_context overrides (budget_constraint) are applied identically on both deterministic and orch-success paths. orch_outcome is unaffected by override application.

request.question: should I sell Saka for Salah

budget_constraint.note: itb=5 (Â£0.5m) is less than any realistic price_delta â€” budget_constraint fires

recommendation.note: recommendation unchanged by budget_constraint â€” always score-based

orch_outcome.override_invariant: orch_outcome value is set before override application and is never modified by _apply_squad_overrides

session_ask_fixtures.description: Single-turn session. Captain score for a known player. Standard routed path. orch_outcome always present in session response.

session_id.note: same session_id as returned by POST /session

orch_outcome.note: always present in session response; null when orch not attempted

rewritten_question.note: null when debug=false or no rewrite occurred

session_ask_fixtures.description: Session turn with valid intent_hint. Bare name routed via hint. Hint is per-turn â€” not persisted in session state.
Second turn in same session WITHOUT intent_hint after a turn that used one. Hint must not persist. 'Haaland' alone has no deterministic route â†’ unsupported_intent.

debug.classification_source.note: hint from prior turn must not carry over

session_ask_fixtures.description: Pronoun follow-up resolved via ConversationSession state. 'him' refers to the player from the previous turn.

body.note: Pronoun resolved deterministically without LLM. Prior turn must establish last_player.

session_ask_fixtures.description: Session not found â€” POST /session/{id}/ask on an unknown or expired session_id.
session_ask_fixtures.precondition: session_id is not in the active session registry

expected.note: HTTP 404 is the contract signal for missing or expired sessions.

probe_fixtures.description: GET /healthz returns routing_counters and graduation sub-dicts. Both keys must be present. routing_counters must have all eight branch keys plus total_primary and reject_rate.
GET /health returns 200 while the process is running with bootstrap loaded. Liveness-only: does not reflect bootstrap readiness.

status.note: /health is liveness-only. Always 200 while the process runs.

probe_fixtures.description: GET /health returns 200 even when bootstrap is not loaded. Confirms liveness-only semantics: the probe passes before data is ready.

status.note: /health is liveness-only. Returns 200 even when /ready would return 503.

probe_fixtures.description: GET /ready returns 200 when bootstrap is loaded. Use this endpoint for deployment readiness probes so traffic is only routed after data is available.

status.note: /ready is the readiness gate. 200 means data is loaded and /ask requests will be served.

probe_fixtures.description: GET /ready returns 503 when bootstrap is not loaded (startup failure, data not yet fetched, or all retry attempts exhausted). No body assertions: 503 is the readiness gate signal.

expected.note: 503 is the readiness gate. Deployment probes should wait for 200 before routing traffic.

http_status_contract.200: Request processed. Inspect 'supported' and 'outcome' in the body for the domain result. HTTP 200 does not imply outcome='ok'.
http_status_contract.404: Session not found or expired. POST /session/{id}/ask and GET /session/{id} only.
http_status_contract.422: Malformed request body â€” missing or invalid 'question' field.
http_status_contract.429: Session cap reached. POST /session only. Clear idle sessions and retry.
http_status_contract.503: Bootstrap not initialised or not yet ready. POST /ask and POST /session/{id}/ask return 503 when bootstrap is None. GET /ready returns 503 until bootstrap is loaded.
